title: 2024 AIME I Problems/Problem 2
problem: There exist real numbers $x$ and $y$ , both greater than 1, such that $\log_x\left(y^x\right)=\log_y\left(x^{4y}\right)=10$ . Find $xy$ .
solution: By properties of logarithms, we can simplify the given equation to $x\log_xy=4y\log_yx=10$ . Let us break this into two separate equations: \[x\log_xy=10\] \[4y\log_yx=10.\] We multiply the two equations to get: \[4xy\left(\log_xy\log_yx\right)=100.\] Also by properties of logarithms, we know that $\log_ab\cdot\log_ba=1$ ; thus, $\log_xy\cdot\log_yx=1$ . Therefore, our equation simplifies to: \[4xy=100\implies xy=\boxed{025}.\] ~Technodoggo
